zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Joan Bert於1530年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可素德波利時，她的父親William Bert34歲，母親Johanna Godbold30歲。她於1559年與Nicholas Dandridge在英國素福爾可素德波利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1591年3月24日在英國素福爾可素德波利享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Joan Bert于1530年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可素德波利时，她的父亲William Bert34岁，母亲Johanna Godbold30岁。她于1559年与Nicholas Dandridge在英国素福爾可素德波利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1591年3月24日在英国素福爾可素德波利享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Berton
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Bert  .
English: variant of   Burton  .

History: Pierre Bert(h)on or Bertin, a Huguenot from the French province of Poitou, took in 1686 the lead of the short-lived Narragansett colony, in RI. His surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (as Pierre Bertin or Bertine or Bertain or Bartain or Berton or Bretin) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America (as Pierre Bertine).

variants.name: Delbert
meanings: French: patronymic from the personal name   Bert  .

variants.name: Berth
meanings: German: variant of   Bert  .

variants.name: Bettencourt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Jorge, Luis, Ana, Carlos, Joaquin, Aires, Arlindo, Armando, Francisco, Mario.  Portuguese Joao, Adao, Albano, Conceicao, Duarte, Ilidio, Paulo, Serafim.

French (northern) and Portuguese (of French origin; mainly Azores): habitational name from any of various places in northern France so called, with minor variations in spelling, of which the main one is in Somme. They are named with the ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ) + Old French court ‘farm(yard)’. This surname is now much more frequent in Portugal than in France; to Portugal it was brought in the 15th century. See also French Bethencourt and Spanish Betancourt .

variants.name: Berteau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Leonce.
French:
from a variant of the personal name Bertaud, from ancient Germanic Berhtwald, composed of the elements berht ‘bright’ and wald ‘power, authority’.

variants.name: Bertram
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Bethune
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin); French (Béthune): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, Picardy, France, recorded in the 8th century in the Latin form Bitunia, probably an adjective (with villa understood) derived from the oblique form of an ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ). See also Scottish Beaton .

variants.name: Bright
meanings: English: usually a nickname from Middle English bright ‘bright, good-looking, fresh-faced’ (Old English beorht ‘bright, shining’).

English: perhaps occasionally from an unrecorded Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Briht, Beorht, from a short form of any of several Old English personal names of which beorht was the first element, such as Beorhtrīc ‘bright’ + ‘powerful’. Compare Bert .
Americanized form of German   Brecht  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Joan Bert was born in 1530, in Sudbury, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Bert, was 34 and her mother, Johanna Godbold, was 30. She married Nicholas Dandridge in 1559, in Sudbury, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 24 March 1591, in Sudbury, Suffolk, England, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Joan Bert est née en 1530, à Sudbury, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Bert, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Johanna Godbold, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas Dandridge en 1559, à Sudbury, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 24 mars 1591, à Sudbury, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Joan Bert 1530 in Sudbury, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Bert 34 und ihre Mutter Johanna Godbold 30. Sie hat 1559 in Sudbury, Suffolk, England Nicholas Dandridge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 24. März 1591 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Sudbury, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Joan Bert è nata il nel 1530 nel Sudbury, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Bert, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Johanna Godbold, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Nicholas Dandridge nel 1559, nel Sudbury, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 24 marzo 1591, nel Sudbury, Suffolk, England, all'età di 61 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Joan Bert(이)가 1530년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 수드버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Bert(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Godbold(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1559년 영국 수폴크 수드버이에서 Nicholas Dandridge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1591년 3월 24일 영국 수폴크 수드버이에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.
